name: AIBuildAnalyzer
description: Analyzes a failed pipeline run with an OpenAI-compatible LLM and attaches the result to the build, where the AI Analyzer tab displays it.
helpMarkDown: Add this task as the last step of the job with `condition: failed()`. Point `llmUrl`/`llmModel` at your own LLM. The result appears in the AI Analyzer tab on the build results page.
author: AI Build Analyzer

inputs.name: llmUrl
inputs.label: LLM base URL (OpenAI-compatible, ending in /v1)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Base URL of any OpenAI-compatible chat-completions API (Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM, llama.cpp, OpenAI, etc.). The build agent must be able to reach this address. Example: http://localhost:11434/v1 for Ollama.

inputs.name: llmModel
inputs.label: Model name
inputs.helpMarkDown: The model identifier as your server expects it, e.g. llama3.1, qwen2.5-coder, gpt-oss:20b, gpt-4o-mini.

inputs.name: llmApiKey
inputs.label: API key (optional)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Sent as a Bearer token if provided. Pass a secret pipeline variable, e.g. $(LLM_API_KEY).

inputs.name: insecureTls
inputs.label: Allow self-signed TLS certificates (curl -k)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enable only if your LLM uses an HTTPS endpoint with a self-signed/internal certificate.

inputs.name: timeoutMs
inputs.label: LLM request timeout (ms)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Increase for large local models that respond slowly.

inputs.name: apiVersion
inputs.label: Azure DevOps REST API version
inputs.helpMarkDown: Match your server: Azure DevOps Services and Server 2022.1+ support 7.1; Server 2022 uses 7.0; Server 2020 uses 6.0; Server 2019 uses 5.0. Lower this if you get HTTP 400 reading logs.
